body:
Daily Message

Dear Friend,

Today is the final
debate before Iowans caucus on Monday. It's an opportunity for
Iowans to hear from each candidate on the stage and to decide for
themselves who is the candidate who best represents their values.

Donald Trump has said
he plans to skip the debate. That is a disservice to the people
of Iowa, and if Donald doesn't want to participate in a debate
with the chosen moderators, Ted Cruz has welcomed Donald to
debate him one-on-one, with or without a moderator, so that
voters can hear from them on their record, their beliefs, and
their promise to the people of Iowa and to people across this
country.

Ducking from the
debate is not becoming of a presidential candidate. If Donald
can't stand up to his competitors on stage, how will he stand up
to Putin and radical Islamic terrorists? We need a leader who is
a proven, principled, and consistent conservative to serve as
president and Commander in Chief.

Best,

Rick Tyler
Communications
Director
www.tedcruz.org

name: Alex Conant, MarcoRubio.com
subject: Marco’s plan for tonight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
body:
| | | | | | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45981615:6tAmPVrpN:m:1:1349778030:E17A093148070B47C062122456545F01:r)
---
| | | | Peter, Every debate this cycle has been important, but we all know this is the biggest one yet. Iowans start picking our next president in just over four days, can you believe that? Here in Des Moines, you can feel the pressure. But believe me, Marco's ready. He's worked incredibly hard (and so have his supporters -- in Iowa and elsewhere) to get to this moment. As with other debates, Marco's going to use tonight to get his message out there -- his conservative policies to build a New American Century, his plans to undo the damage President Obama's done to our country, and how he'll unify the Republican party. That'll set him apart from some other candidates: Some are going to come in aiming just to tear each other down. Some of them don't have conservative records to tout. Some of them are all about dividing America, and the Republican party, into hostile factions. Marco did not come to Des Moines to attack other Republicans \-- but he'll be ready to respond if they attack him. He will not hesitate to point out the differences between himself and other candidates, but his message is fundamentally a positive one. The Republican party must unite behind a candidate who will beat Hillary and build a New American Century. We're not going to do that with a message that divides America -- we're going to do it with Marco's optimism. We want to show Marco all the support we can get before he takes the stage tonight -- [**will you go here to chip in to keep his momentum going?**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45981615:6tAmPVrpN:m:1:1349778030:E17A093148070B47C062122456545F01:r) And tune in to Fox News at 9 PM Eastern tonight -- you won't want to miss it. subject: Fwd: I'm running for President
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carson America, Inc
body:
---
| |

Peter,

I've been going through some old emails and I wanted to share this one with you because it means a lot to me. It's the original announcement of my candidacy from May 4th of last year.

You see, tonight's Fox News debate at 9pm ET will be my last opportunity to make my case to a national audience before the Iowa Caucuses on February 1st.

On that day, the race to replace President Obama will be officially underway. That's just 4 days away -- and I wanted to remind you why this election is so important, and why I believe I am best suited to serve as your next Commander-in-Chief.

**[Please scroll down to read it now, and then help us win by making a donation of just $4.](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r)**

I may not be leading in the polls like I once was, but we're in a strong position. Iowa is known for breaking late and we're seeing real momentum on the ground. We've built a massive, grassroots Get-Out-The-Vote operation across the entire state and we are running as many TV ads as we can afford.

[**With 4 days to go, **](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r)**[your donation of just $4 right now can put us over the top.](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r)**
**
**Thank you, and I hope you'll tune in tonight.

-Ben

Sent from my iPhone

Begin forwarded message:

**From:** Ben Carson
**Subject:** I'm running for President
**Date:** May 04, 2015
**To:** Ben Carson

[](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r)
---

Peter, I've got a big announcement that I'd like to share with you.

**I am running for President of the United States, and [I ask for your support](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r).**

If this great nation is to survive the challenges of the modern world, we need to heal, we need to be inspired, and we need to revive the exceptional spirit that built America.

**Working with you and all of our fellow citizens, I want to lead that revival.**

I am not a politician, nor am I politically correct.

But I believe my values, my life experience, and my willingness to speak the truth and seek solutions prepares me well to lead our nation toward more prosperity, security, and freedom for every American.**

[So I ask for your commitment, right now, to this campaign. On Day One, I ask you to be among the first to make a contribution to Carson America, and I will be forever grateful](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r)**.

[](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r)
---

Never before have we been so closely connected to each other, but more divided as a country.

Our political class has failed us, and as a nation we must work to reestablish what Abraham Lincoln once called a government of, by and for the people.

**While I know it will not be easy, I truly look forward to the journey ahead, and [I hope you will join me](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/33167061:WRKV9UyNd:m:1:876315805:2CFA59E8ACA5FE19621B418645236282:r).**

Thank you, and God Bless America.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

body:
CRUZ FOR PRESIDENT

We have been trying to get a hold of you...

Friend, this is of the utmost importance.

Here in the office, we have been trying to get a hold of you to
ask for your immediate help -- I'm emailing you now because I
have this written down as the best way to reach you...

If this email reaches you, please respond ASAP. Ted has asked me
to reach you immediately -- he is counting on you, and I really
hope you can respond right now.

He's been trying to reach you about the situation developing in
Iowa, and with the Iowa Caucus in just three days, it has reached
CRITICAL importance.

We're falling behind. See the spreadsheet below.

I'm sorry to say -- those numbers are correct.

That means we're more than $149,000 dollars short of what we need
to fund our Iowa volunteers for Caucus day on Monday.

At Ted's request, I'm asking you for a personal favor -- will you
make one last EMERGENCY donation to the campaign?

In my daily report to Ted tomorrow, I need to tell him that we've
made up the difference.

Your $5 or $35 NEEDS to be at the top of that list, or I think we
may come up short.

First thing tomorrow, I'll make sure Ted knows that you donated.
I'm sure you can see now why I've been so desperate to connect
with you.

I'm doing everything I can, but with all we've worked so hard for
coming down to the next three days, I need some serious help.
Will you stop whatever you're in the middle of and make a
donation -- any donation -- to help us win Iowa?
You have no idea how much it will mean to the campaign.

- Lauren

name: Joe Sestak
subject: No better time than yesterday for a moratorium on fracking
body:
[](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719120/3476a20e/c4310b2/2744cb81/599239004/VEsH/ "http://joesestak.com")
---

**Open Op-Ed: No better time than yesterday for a moratorium on fracking** By: Joe Sestak | [](http://action.joesestak.com/page/m/1719120/3476a20e/c4310b2/2744cb80/599239004/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/admiraljoesestak-web?refcode=16EM0127FBb1&amounts=25,50,100,250,500,1000,2700")
---
During my 31 years in the U.S. Navy, I saw firsthand some of the most environmentally sensitive places on the planet and the consequences of environmental and conservation failures.
And as a Pennsylvanian born and raised, I well-know the lessons of the demise of the anthracite coal industry, where the Commonwealth was left with a $15 billion cleanup of 2,500 miles of damaged streams and 250,000 acres of contaminated land because of the lack of adequate regulation and the absence of proper oversight.
That's why when I returned to Pennsylvania and was elected to serve as a Congressman, I fought to try and ensure that my fellow Pennsylvanians would not have to sacrifice their clean water, clean air, natural lands, and local roads and infrastructure in the hasty pursuit of Marcellus shale gas.
I called for a moratorium on Marcellus shale fracking because I was not convinced we had strong enough environmental, health, and property safeguards, and I was not satisfied that people would have the access to just compensation should even the best safeguards fail.
As I said last week to the statewide press corps at the Pennsylvania Press Club in Harrisburg, my position remains the same today because it was based on the facts then - almost all of which remain true now.
For example, fracking still falls under the so-called "Halliburton Loophole" that was slipped into President Bush's energy bill in 2005 and allows energy companies to ignore the rules of the federal Safe Drinking Water Act.
Safe water protections exist for a reason, and fracking involves using huge amounts of water laced with undisclosed chemicals that continue to contaminate drinking water across Pennsylvania.
It's why I cosponsored the FRAC Act to close this loophole, and why I wrote a letter to the Administrator of the EPA about the dangers of these drilling operations which I said were expanding in Pennsylvania at an unprecedented rate and scale without proper protections for the health of Pennsylvanians.
Unfortunately, since the very beginning of the Marcellus shale boom, too many of our leaders and regulators let the voice of the shale industry drown out the desires of Pennsylvanians to have safe water, and too many argued for industry interests to the detriment of Pennsylvanians.
The Halliburton Loophole remains wide open, and people's health has remained inadequately protected. It is why I remain concerned today about the still-unknown extent of the damage fracking has already done to our Commonwealth.
Just last May, scientists finally linked a key chemical of fracking fluid to water samples taken in 2012 from three wells in Bradford County.
In August, the Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection finished an almost four year investigation in Lycoming and Tioga counties, conclusively determining that methane gas from natural gas wells had migrated off-site and impacted private drinking water wells serving homes in the region.
And in October, at least five private water supplies in Potter County were polluted with drilling additives and a surfactant fluid known as "F-485" and "Rock Drill Oil 150 (0428)."
The people have suffered, while the fracking companies and their consultants prosper from our shared natural resources.
Moreover, hydraulic fracturing would not be so successful today if it were not for our shared investment as citizens in the development of the industry. It was the Department of Energy (DOE) - using our tax dollars - that sponsored the initial industry research to estimate the volume of natural gas in shale reservoirs.
And it was our taxpayer investment in the DOE that developed the diamond-tipped drill bits for geothermal energy that were then used by the natural gas industry. Even the Navy sonar we created to track Russian submarines was repurposed at great taxpayer expense for drillers to track shale deposits.
Without the above, frackers would not be in Pennsylvania today.
It's just one more way in which the shale boom has been unfair to Pennsylvanians: we have not seen a fair return on our investment that created the industry, because of opposition by consultants and lobbyists to a tax on the wealth that is coming from fracking.
Yes, we need a moratorium until health and environmental concerns are fully addressed, but we should still not lift it until there is a fair severance tax put in place - after all, we paid for its development.
Our citizens shouldn't have to bear the burden of the increased cost to our infrastructure, risks to our health and safety, and any ancillary environmental degradation due to the fracking industry.
While I wish we would have had a moratorium when I called for one years ago, it is also worth noting that there is no better time than now - economically - to put one in place.
Because the rise in supply of natural gas has exceeded the rise in demand - and as efficiency has increased as well - the price of natural gas in December is the lowest it has been since 1994.
In fact, this month's edition of the Oil & Gas Journal cited a Moody's study to predict that oversupply this year "will continue to weigh down" the U.S. natural gas market leading to "capital spending reductions of at least 20-25% in 2016 across the exploration and production business."
There is no better time than yesterday for a moratorium, which is why it is imperative that we act today.
---

subject: A tale of two campaigns
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
Sisters and Brothers -
Yesterday provided one of the clearest contrasts between our grassroots political revolution and Hillary Clinton's campaign.
In Mason City, Iowa, Bernie spoke to a packed house of 1,100 committed supporters and caucus goers about how our movement is the best hope to take our country back from the billionaire class.
Hillary Clinton left Iowa yesterday, choosing to court contributions from one of the many financial investment firms supporting her campaign instead of spending valuable time with caucus goers.
Our campaign is powered by a dedicated movement of volunteers and almost 3 million grassroots contributions. Hillary Clinton's day off of the trail is a clear indication that, against all odds, they are finding it difficult to keep up with us. And that is entirely because of supporters like you. Let's keep up the pressure.
[**Make a $3 contribution to our campaign to help us win in Iowa Monday night. Together, we can prove that when people stand together, anything is possible.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2c33/43f17890/41bcebc9/620e1bdf/2299235057/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/before-iowa-caucus?refcode=em160128am-sub-a")
When we started this campaign, the political and media establishment of this country laughed at us. They said our "radical ideas" like universal health care, free college tuition, and getting big money out of politics were pie-in-the-sky idealism. You still hear some of that today.
But we have a chance to send an unmistakable message about the strength of our movement in Iowa, New Hampshire and beyond. We made that happen together.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016

name: Rand Paul
subject: How America will thrive
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Patriot,

Today I had a chance to speak with Drake
University to speak with students and supporters in Iowa.

It was great to once again bring my message
of liberty straight to the voters and share my vision for
America.

America is still exceptional and is still a
beacon for the world.

But the American experiment with Liberty is
not totally won. Today, tomorrow, and the day after that, we
must fight to restrain Big Brother.

Will you stand with me?

Liberty is under assault like never before.

In Washington and on the campaign trail,
Republicans and Democrats alike call out for bigger government.

Only a President who understands the
corrupting influence of Big Government can stop it! I believe
that with your help, I can do that.

Right and Left spend too much money and
carelessly infringe on your civil liberties… both parties
contribute to the assault on the Bill of Rights.

The Left attacks your Second Amendment right
to bear arms and the
Right attacks your Fourth Amendment privacy
rights.

I am the only candidate who stands for the
ENTIRE Bill of Rights!

I see an America strong enough deter foreign
aggression yet wise enough to avoid unnecessary intervention.

I see a simplified flat tax that unburdens
our citizens from the fear and intimidation of the IRS tax code.
I would unleash the engine of capitalism to create jobs and
opportunity like never before.

I see an America where criminal justice is
applied equally and any law that is disaproportionately applied
to people of color is repealed.

I see our big cities once again shining and
beckoning with creativity and ingenuity, with American companies
offering American jobs.

I have a vision for an America beyond
partisan squabbling, beyond petty divisions.

And I believe we will thrive when we finally
believe in ourselves again.

Will you stand with me today to make that
America a reality?

In liberty,
Rand Paul

name: Team Jeb
subject: RE: Heading into Monday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeb 2016, Inc
body:

body:
Dear Patriot,

In just minutes, I will be walking out on
stage for the final Republican debate before voting begins.

I'm prepared. I'm ready. I can already hear
the restless crowd. And I'm prepared to go all-in with my
message of liberty.

But before I walk out, I must ask you a
personal favor.

Patriot, will you help me reach my $250,000
fundraising goal with your most generous contribution of $50, $25
or even $10 right away?

Tonight's debate kicks off a furious final
three days of campaigning before voting begins on February 1.

I'm running for President because I believe
our Constitution and our Bill of Rights are precious. I'm
running because I believe we must not forget who we are.

I'm running because I'm worried about where
we're headed as a country, and I wonder how much of the freedom
most Americans have always taken for granted will still be there
for our kids and grandkids.

I could not be more proud of the progress
you've helped my campaign make thus far.

With 1,000 precinct captains and nearly
900,000 phone calls made to Iowa voters, we have the
organization. I'm proud to say we're closing in on our goal of
10,000 pledged supporters on college campuses.

Now, I have this debate and three days of
feverish campaigning left.

I'm ready to shock the world.

Will you please stand with me? Will you
please agree to your most generous gift of $50, $25 or even $10
right away?

In liberty,
Rand Paul

name: Team Bernie
subject: What we've done together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
When we started this campaign, no one gave us a chance. But then 6,000 people showed up at our announcement speech in Burlington. Our crowds swelled to 10,000 in Madison, 15,000 in Seattle and 27,500 in Los Angeles, California.
Our volunteer base grew larger than any other campaign. At the same time, we rose in the polls nationally and trimmed Hillary Clinton's lead down to single digits in Iowa.

But out of every contribution we've received -- none is more important than the one you can make right now, because tomorrow is the last day we can put it to work to win Iowa.

body:
What Iowa meant in 2008. | Friend --

Anyone who worked on President Obama's 2008 campaign can tell you the importance of the Iowa caucus. Winning helped catapult us forward toward the nomination -- and the White House. A loss could have set us back.

With the Iowa polls this tight and the Sanders campaign outspending Hillary on the airwaves, every $1 matters. A lot of these precincts will come down to just a few people, which means valuable delegates can swing one way or the other based on the tiniest of margins -- it’s critical to reach as many Iowans as possible in these final days.

**Your $1 could make the difference. Be a part of history, and help Hillary win in Iowa:** | [ | [**Donate $1**](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MjQ1ODcxNTYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzI0MTE1NjU1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

Thank you,

David Plouffe
